project_type: Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript app ("harness") that turns a natural-language request into an Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) build plan and executes it via MCP tool calls
build_system: npm (package.json scripts: dev/build/start/lint). No Makefile/Dockerfile. node v22.22.3 available via nvm (`source /opt/toolchains/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22`).
test_framework: None installed. No test runner in package.json/devDependencies, no describe/test/it blocks anywhere in lib/**. Verification must be done via `npm run build` (tsc type-check through Next.js) plus an ad-hoc script run with `npx tsx` (or `node --loader ts-node/esm`, but tsx is simpler and works without extra config) that imports lib/plan-builder.ts's planUseCase()/classifyUseCase() directly and asserts module inclusion for representative SolutionConfig inputs.
build_command: npm install (first time only) && npm run build
test_command: No existing test command. Use a one-off script executed via `npx tsx scripts/<name>.ts` (create under a temp/scripts location, can be deleted after verification, or kept if useful) that calls planUseCase() from lib/plan-builder.ts with representative SolutionConfig objects and logs which modules were included, to confirm gating behavior. Also run `npm run build` for type-checking and `npm run lint` if fast.
verification_instructions: 1) npm install to get node_modules (none present yet). 2) npm run build must succeed (Next.js type-checks all .ts/.tsx via tsconfig). 3) Write and run a small tsx/node script that calls planUseCase(config) for at least 3 representative configs: (a) schema-only ask (domain set, events=[], segments=[], destinations=[], personalization_placements=[], goals=[]) -> expect ONLY 'rag' + 'aep' modules included, NOT launch/cja/ajo_activation/ajo_offers; (b) full activation ask (events + segments + an email/push/sms/web destination) -> expect launch + cja + ajo_activation included as appropriate; (c) analytics-only ask (segments present, destinations=[] or explicitly CRM/reporting goal, no activation channel and no explicit request for outbound campaigns) -> current heuristic previously treated an EMPTY destinations array as 'activation assumed by default' for ajo_activation - this must change so an empty destinations array does NOT by itself trigger AJO or Launch; only explicit signal (real events for Launch, explicit activation-channel destinations or explicit user intent for AJO) should gate them on. Print the ModulePlanSummary list for each and confirm by eye / assertion that the module set matches expectation.
setup_instructions: source /opt/toolchains/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22 (or use default v22.22.3 already active). Then `npm install` in /projects/sandbox/NEXT_HARNESS. No .env or .env.local present - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and MCP_ENDPOINT_URL are both unset, so the LLM synthesis path in lib/llm-planner.ts cannot be exercised live and the API route cannot be run end-to-end against a real MCP backend. Do not attempt to set these or call the live API route; verify the LLM-path changes (prompt text in buildSynthesisPrompt, and any catalog/validation scope guard) by reading the code / running any pure-function helpers directly, not by making live Anthropic API calls.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode, so `npm install` can reach the npm registry. No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or MCP_ENDPOINT_URL configured in this sandbox, so the LLM synthesis path and real MCP tool calls cannot be exercised live - verification of the deterministic heuristic path (lib/plan-builder.ts) via direct function calls is the primary verification method. No test framework is installed; do not add a heavy new one unless truly needed - prefer a plain script run via `npx tsx`.
contribution_requirements: AGENTS.md states: this Next.js version has breaking changes vs. training data - read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing Next.js-specific code. This task only touches plain lib/*.ts modules (no App Router files), so that constraint is lower-risk here, but do not touch app/ routing conventions carelessly. No CONTRIBUTING.md exists.
key_patterns: Capability-module pattern in lib/plan-builder.ts: MODULES array of CapabilityModule objects, each with isApplicable(config)/reason(config)/priority(config,useCase)/build(config,ctx). planUseCase() filters+sorts+concatenates. classifyUseCase(config) derives a UseCaseProfile from destinations/goals/placements/events/segments, used both for module priority ordering and (for cja/ajo_activation) for isApplicable itself. intent-extractor.ts's enrichConfigFromDescription() re-scans the raw description with regex keyword tables and ADDS (never removes) events/segments/placements/goals/destinations on top of the MCP planner's regex output, then llm-planner.ts's planBuildAsync() runs that enrichment first, then optionally LLM-synthesizes a plan (validated against lib/tool-catalog.ts's TOOL_CATALOG allowlist via validateSynthesizedSteps()), falling back to planUseCase() heuristic on any failure.
relevant_files: lib/plan-builder.ts - MODULES definitions, isApplicable/priority logic for aepModule/launchModule/cjaModule/ajoActivationModule/ajoOffersModule, classifyUseCase(), planUseCase(). Primary fix location.

lib/intent-extractor.ts - enrichConfigFromDescription()/extractIntent() regex keyword tables that add events/segments/placements/goals/destinations from the raw description; review whether any keyword is too permissive (e.g. broad event keywords implicitly satisfying launchModule.isApplicable for narrow asks).
lib/llm-planner.ts - buildSynthesisPrompt() text instructing the LLM what to include; planBuildAsync() flow.
lib/tool-catalog.ts - TOOL_CATALOG allowlist + validateSynthesizedSteps(); currently has no category/scope restriction.
lib/types.ts - SolutionConfig, PlanningInfo/UseCaseProfile types (for reference only, unlikely to need changes).
app/api/build/route.ts - caller of planBuildAsync(), for reference only, unlikely to need changes.
directory_structure: Flat lib/ directory holds all planning/execution logic (plan-builder.ts, intent-extractor.ts, llm-planner.ts, tool-catalog.ts, execution-runner.ts, execution-store.ts, mcp-client.ts, observability.ts, types.ts). app/api/build/route.ts is the only API route that drives planning. No tests/ or __tests__/ directory exists. Root has many *.md docs (MCP_TOOLS_REFERENCE.md, PLANNER_KEYWORDS.md, HARNESS_*.md) describing intended architecture and the upstream MCP's regex-based planner_parse_natural_language tool, which the harness cannot modify (lives in a separate MCP repo, not this one).
